The slice shows ownership transfer to a persistent data structure rather than a local leak, so the NeverFree path within this function is not a real vulnerability. Such NASM group/segment lists are torn down during backend cleanup, so the allocation is not lost on this path. The reported site is an ownership transfer into a long-lived data structure, not a never-freed local pointer.
